violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2024

Milk, cream, and oat milk were observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the beverage servers. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, items had been on the counter for about 1 hour. Items were relocated to a refrigeration unit to cool. Food must not be stored out of refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Ice machine must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 09/21/2023
